Privileged & Confidential — Internal Compliance Memorandum (Attorneys’ Eyes Only)
From: Office of the General Counsel, NorthHarbor HealthBank, Inc. (NASDAQ-listed U.S. issuer; healthcare + banking) — contact legal@northharborhealth.example, 555-0148
To: Board Audit & Compliance Committee; CC: Investor Relations, HR, CISO — Date: 2026-05-31 — Re: Reg FD remediation and PII/PHI/GLBA handling
Summary: On 5/28, Mr. Cade Mercer (VP, Corporate Development) discussed non-public Q2 clinic attrition and MarlinPay card charge-off trends with Ms. Tamsin Yule, an analyst at Fencrest Securities, ahead of our scheduled 6/3 earnings call.
This communication, coupled with a draft board deck sent via personal email from cmercer@nhhb-internal.example, likely constitutes selective disclosure under Reg FD and raises potential Rule 10b-5 risk if any trading ensued; file an Item 7.01 Form 8-K with a broad press release.
The draft also contained anchored PII: Mr. Dorian Vale (Loan Ops Analyst, Employee ID HV-44219) — undergoing treatment for Type 1 diabetes; CYP2C19 poor-metabolizer genetic result on file; and registered steward of Finance Workers Local 744.
Additionally, Mr. Vale’s fingerprint template used for the Bayford vault timeclock (biometric) and his private calendar entry noting Sabbath observance accommodations were viewable in the shared link, implicating HIPAA, CCPA, and labor-law sensitivities.
For clarity, biometric locks protect the data room; this sentence does not describe any person’s biometric data.
A second attachment included Ms. Kira Hollis’s patient-financing worksheet showing her minor child’s care plan number (no SSN) and PulsePort EHR encounter codes, which must be removed before any Reg FD corrective posting.
Recommended actions: file a same-day 8-K furnishing the high-level revenue range without patient details; issue a public statement; and quarantine the board materials per Policy FIN-102 (Insider Communications) and HIPAA SOP PR-17.
Until the 8-K posts, restrict outreach to covered market actors; use the pre-cleared Q&A; do not “move the goalposts” with new metrics; all analyst questions to be routed to investorrelations@northharborhealth.example.
CISO to rotate the Bayford SSO keys, purge the Chronis biometric cache for Mr. Vale pending a fresh consent, and validate GLBA Reg S-P notices on the MarlinPay portfolio.
HR to document accommodations and remove religious and union annotations from role-based calendar exposures; Legal to sequester genetic reports in the ClinSpec vault with access logging under Policy PS-22.
Note: References to community health or political climate in our DEI town hall are generic; we support inclusion, equity, and belonging, and the Riverton Rockets winning streak does not affect disclosure controls.
We will not name counterparties in the potential VeridianCare JV in external materials until definitive agreements are executed; internal metaphor: “skate to where the puck will be,” but avoid sharing roadmaps titled BlueCedar-9 outside the Clean Team.
